mechanism_narrative: A high and stable federal funds rate, following a tightening cycle, acts as a drag on economic activity by increasing borrowing costs for corporations and consumers. This restrictive stance compresses profit margins and lowers equity valuations over a multi-month horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has held rates at 5.25% since June 2006 after 17 consecutive hikes.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Sharpe ratio is positive over any 3-year rolling window when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in housing starts from cyclical peaks is a leading indicator of a broad-based economic slowdown. This weakness spreads through wealth effects and reduced construction activity, negatively impacting S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST has fallen from a peak over 2200 in early 2006 to below 1700, a significant contraction.
failure_condition: A negative 12-month change in HOUST is not followed by below-average S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60-day period in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate begins to rise from a cyclical low while monetary policy remains restrictive (high FEDFUNDS), it signals that the economy is tipping into recession. This interaction creates a strong risk-off signal for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses a similar unemployment-based logic for recession detection.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal remains below 0.03 over a full business cycle walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing momentum in industrial production indicates weakening business activity and capacity utilization. This directly impacts corporate revenue growth and earnings outlook, leading to negative pressure on stock prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a core component of many leading economic indexes.
failure_condition: Periods of negative 3-month change in INDPRO are not associated with statistically significant underperformance of the S&P 500 over the next 20 days (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation in the context of an already high Fed Funds rate creates a stagflationary risk. The Fed is constrained from easing policy to support growth, which forces a painful adjustment through lower corporate earnings and compressed equity multiples.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Stronger-than-expected growth in nonfarm payrolls is a real-time indicator of economic resilience. This positive surprise boosts investor sentiment and expectations for near-term corporate revenue, driving positive short-term returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly employment report is a major market-moving data release.
failure_condition: The top quintile of monthly PAYEMS changes is not associated with positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 days more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A significant slowdown in retail sales growth points to consumer retrenchment, which is a major headwind for the U.S. economy. This weakening demand translates directly to lower corporate sales and triggers downward revisions to earnings estimates.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending accounts for approximately two-thirds of U.S. GDP.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate a negative hit rate (returns < 0) greater than 55% in a 10-year backtest when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) confirms a healthy, expanding economy. This fundamental backdrop supports aggregate corporate earnings growth and justifies stable to rising equity market valuations over the subsequent quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP is the broadest measure of economic output.
failure_condition: Quarters with positive real GDP growth are not followed by a positive average 60-day S&P 500 return over a full business cycle.
